summary: This topic explains how to create and define new subjects within the ERP system. It covers entering subject names, unique codes, selecting assessment models, and optionally restricting subjects to specific classes for mapping purposes.
steps: Navigate to the 'Academics' section via the 'Settings' icon.
Locate and click on the 'Add Subject' option.
Enter a unique 'Subject Code' (e.g., ENG). This code defines the subject and is mandatory.
Select an 'Assessment Model' (e.g., Scholastic Subject, Course Scholastic Subject).
Optionally, select specific 'Class and Stream' if the subject should only be available for mapping in those particular classes.
Click 'Add' or 'Save' to create the subject.
business_rules: Subject Code is a mandatory field and must be unique.
If a subject has already been added in class mapping, its original name cannot be edited directly from the subject definition; only its display name can be changed within the mapping.
Subject codes define the subject itself, not the class (e.g., 'ENG' for English is consistent across all grades).
important_notes: Assessment Models: 'Scholastic' subjects are for marks entry, while 'Course Scholastic' (or similar) might use grading criteria.
Subjects can be pre-filtered to appear only for specific classes during the subject-class mapping process.
questions: How do I add a new subject to the ERP system?
What is the purpose of a subject code and is it mandatory?
What are the different assessment models for subjects?
Can I restrict a subject to only be available for certain classes?
How do I edit a subject's name after it has been mapped to a class?
related_topics: Teacher Mapping for Classes and Subjects
